title: Human Placenta Tissue Collection and Preservation Methods - UCSD Female Reproductive TMC
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5mvg9","text":"Human Placenta Tissue collection and storage protocol for HuBMAP's UCSD Female Reproductive TMC.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3toog","text":"Sterile surgical dissecting instruments (scissors/forceps)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1bi1r","text":"Gauze pad","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"26hk7","text":"Petri dish","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49acg","text":"Sterile 1X PBS (cold, from fridge)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"10l0i","text":"Liquid Nitrogen ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cvk3n","text":"Empty microfuge tubes x9","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"48sl7","text":"Microfuge tubes pre-filled with RNA later x9","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gs03","text":"10% Formalin filled tubes x2","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"enpdl","text":"MACS tissue buffer filled tubes x4 or OCT compound and cryomolds x4 ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: d. Lower the mold slowly into liquid nitrogen until the whole block freezes, then store in -80C freezer
Collect placenta from delivery room within 1 hour of delivery.
Place placenta into bucket and photograph prior to sampling.

Select 2 sites (A and B) from which samples will be collected. Aim for sites to be equidistant from the cord, avoid the edges, avoid areas with fibrin deposits/infarcts/necrosis and/or large blood vessels.
From site A, cut a circle all the way down from the fetal to the maternal surface, about one inch in diameter.
Wash the full-thickness core in cold PBS in a petri dish. Keep the fetal surface pointing up.
Divide the full core in half vertically, from fetal to maternal surface.
With the left half of site A, divide the core horizontally into fetal (F), central (C) and maternal (M) regions.

Cut each of the three regions from the previous step into 6 small chunks for a total of 18 chunks. Chunks should be small enough to just cover the bottom of a microfuge tube (~5mm x 5mm x 5mm). Collect tissue directly adjacent to the fetal and maternal membranes but do not cut from the membranes themselves.

With the right half of site A, divide the tissue into 4 quadrants so that you have 2 fetal chunks and 2 maternal chunks, keeping all the membranes intact. Some of the central region tissue may need to be trimmed off if the core is very thick.
Place one fetal piece and one maternal piece into the 10% formalin tubes (to be processed into FFPE blocks). Place the other fetal and maternal piece into cold MACS tissue buffer. Store in 4C fridge.
Alternatively, if not collecting into MACS tissue buffer, freeze the other fetal and maternal piece in OCT.
Fill plastic cryomold halfway with OCT compound being careful not to create bubbles.
Place fetal tissue (membrane side pointing up) in one mold and maternal tissue (membrane side pointing down) in another mold.
Cover tissue with a thin layer of OCT compound being careful not to create bubbles.
Repeat Steps 4-10 with placenta Site B.
Slowly lower the mold into liquid nitrogen until the whole block freezes, then store in -80C freezer.
Take photos of the placenta after both sites (A and B) are removed. Measure the distance from the cord insertion to each sampling site.
Dab the small chunks on a sterile gauze pad and place each one into a labeled microfuge tube.
Place 9 small chunks (3 fetal, 3 central, 3 maternal) into the empty tubes to be snap frozen. Drop tubes into liquid Nitrogen. Leave for ~2-10 minutes. Remove and store in -80C freezer.

Place the other 9 chunks (3 fetal, 3 central, 3 maternal) into the RNAlater filled tubes. Place RNAlater tubes into a 4C fridge. Allow to sit for 24-48 hours, then remove the RNAlater with a sterile transfer pipette and store in -80C freezer.
creator: Valentina Stanley
